user_query:
I’m a strict referee who wants to stay ahead of any official rule or disciplinary policy changes so I can enforce fair play consistently. Please create a weekly scheduled task that runs locally and does all of the following:

Inputs (must be used):
- input/watchlist.yaml: List of organizations, topics, and their official domains to constrain searches.
- input/baseline.json: Baseline years to compare against when scanning for newer editions or updates.

What the scheduled task must do on each run (using the current local date in ISO YYYY-MM-DD):
1) For each watch item in input/watchlist.yaml, perform a web search restricted to the watch item’s official domain. Use the search text: "{org} {topic}" and constrain results to the specified domain. Collect the top 5 results and save a raw snapshot for each watch item to:
   - data/raw/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<org>_<topic_slug>_search.json
   Each JSON must contain an array of 0–5 objects with fields: title (string), snippet (string, if available; empty string otherwise), url (string), retrieved_at (ISO timestamp), org (string), topic (string), domain (string).
   Notes:
   - Use a general-purpose web search engine of your choice; do not require API keys or credentials.
   - If no results are found or the Internet is unavailable, still produce a valid JSON file with an empty array and continue.

2) From only the saved raw JSON files, compute per watch item:
   - total_results (count of items in the array)
   - update_keyword_hits (count of items where title or snippet contains any of: update, amendment, revision, new, release; case-insensitive)
   - percent_update_mentions (update_keyword_hits / max(total_results, 1), rounded to two decimals)
   - latest_year (the greatest 4-digit year between 2000 and 2099 found in any title or snippet; null if none)
   - baseline_year (looked up via baseline.json using the item’s baseline_key from watchlist.yaml)
   - status ("ahead" if latest_year > baseline_year; otherwise "same_or_behind"; if latest_year is null, treat as "same_or_behind")
   Write these aggregates to a single CSV at:
   - data/aggregates/<YYYY-MM-DD>/watch_stats.csv
   with columns in this exact order: org, topic, total_results, update_keyword_hits, percent_update_mentions, latest_year, baseline_year, status.

3) Compose a concise weekly report at:
   - reports/weekly_discipline_watch_<YYYY-MM-DD>.md
   The report must include:
   - Title line: "Weekly Rule & Discipline Watch — <YYYY-MM-DD>"
   - A summary table or list covering each watch item with the metrics above.
   - A "Potential Updates to Review" section listing any items with status == "ahead" OR percent_update_mentions >= 0.40.
   - A short appendix that lists the title and URL of each captured result per watch item.

4) Scheduling and idempotency:
   - Provide a cron-compatible schedule file at schedule/discipline_watch.cron with a single crontab line that runs the job every Monday at 07:00 local time, writing logs to logs/discipline_watch_<YYYY-MM-DD>.log (you can assume the script determines the date and log path internally).
   - Deliver an executable script in the workspace (your choice of language) that performs steps (1)–(3) on each run. The scheduled line in schedule/discipline_watch.cron must invoke this script.
   - The script must be idempotent per day: re-running on the same date overwrites that day’s data/raw, data/aggregates, report, and log files rather than duplicating entries.

Additional requirements:
- All aggregates must be derived solely from the saved raw JSON snapshots to ensure reproducibility.
- Use the exact directory and file names specified above.
- Keep logs in logs/discipline_watch_<YYYY-MM-DD>.log capturing start/end times and counts per watch item.

Deliverables to create in the workspace:
- schedule/discipline_watch.cron
- The executable script that implements the task (location is your choice, but ensure the cron line references it correctly)
- The first run’s outputs under data/raw/<YYYY-MM-DD>/, data/aggregates/<YYYY-MM-DD>/, reports/weekly_discipline_watch_<YYYY-MM-DD>.md, and logs/discipline_watch_<YYYY-MM-DD>.log

I will verify this by checking that: (a) your script reads input/watchlist.yaml and input/baseline.json; (b) the raw JSON files have the required fields; (c) the CSV metrics are consistent with the raw JSON; (d) the markdown report contains the required sections; and (e) the cron file has the correct schedule and script reference. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
